title: elaeocarpus pedunculatus
intro: Elaeocarpus pedunculatus, also known as the Blue Pepper Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae, native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus pedunculatus include the potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. The herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
